reason: historical attempt import from vela-science/erdos-frontier@121807e20b06eb8148f594dfe13e440181252b0f:attack/attempt-ledger.v2.json (provenance, not a verdict)

attempt.claim: WIN #2 (fully verified): improved Erdos #302 upper bound to f(N) <= 12517/14400 N ~= 0.869236 N, beating our own 5203/5952 ~= 0.874160 and the published 9/10. Independent ILP min-hitting-set recomputation of the deletion vector confirms it.
attempt.detail: Same triple-only weighted-deletion-over-disjoint-dilations method, richer base n=7560=2^3*3^3*5*7. D=divisors(7560)\{1} (63 vertices, 258 triple-edges); independently recomputed the prefix min-vertex-cover (deletion) vector via scipy MILP min-hitting-set -- MATCHES GPT exactly. Weighted sum W=269/540. Dilation set M={v2=0mod4, v3=0mod4, v5,v7 even}, density delta(M)=(8/15)(27/40)(5/6)(7/8)=21/80 (derived from scratch); dilates pairwise disjoint (verified). Forced deletion 21/80*269/540=1883/14400, so f(N) <= 1-1883/14400 = 12517/14400. Lower bound unchanged at 5N/8 + (1/4)N loglog N/log N. Exact values f(N)/N trend ~0.83 (N=100..800), far above 5/8, below 0.869 -- true constant almost certainly >>5/8 but unproven. The certificate is SYSTEMATICALLY improvable by enriching the divisor base (1680->7560 gained 0.0049); a denser base or global LP pushes further.
attempt.reusable_for: Erdos #302 upper bound 12517/14400 (2nd improvement, beats published 9/10); triple-only weighted deletion over disjoint dilations, base-optimizable. Vela verifies each certificate end-to-end (ILP deletion vector + exact density + disjointness).

caveats: A signed banked attempt. Trust is earned via its verifier_attachments + the gate, never from claimed_status.
